disclaimer: General guidance for Surf City HB — not legal or medical advice. Verify rules at official sources.

ebike.q: What do Class 1, 2, and 3 e-bikes mean?
ebike.a: Class 1 is pedal-assist only (motor stops at 20 mph). Class 2 adds a throttle but still caps at 20 mph. Class 3 is pedal-assist up to 28 mph — illegal for riders under 16 in California.

ebike.q: Does a 12-year-old need a helmet on an e-bike?
ebike.a: Yes. California requires riders under 18 to wear an ASTM- or CPSC-approved helmet on streets and bikeways.

ebike.q: How fast can you ride on Huntington Beach paths?
ebike.a: Beach bicycle paths and city parks are 10 mph max — 5 mph when pedestrians are nearby. Many e-bikes can go faster, so speed limiting and route choice matter.

UL 2849 (or related UL listings) means the battery system was tested for electrical and fire safety. It reduces — but does not eliminate — battery fire risk. Always charge per manufacturer instructions.
Pedal-assist boosts you only while pedaling. Throttle lets you ride without pedaling (Class 2). Throttle-only riding can drain the battery faster and may be restricted on some paths.

scooter.q: Are e-scooters legal for a 12-year-old in California?
scooter.a: Motorized scooters can be ridden on bikeways where local rules allow, but riders under 18 must wear a helmet. Sidewalk rules vary — in Huntington Beach, slow down on paths and check HBPD guidance.

scooter.q: Why does UL certification matter on scooters?
scooter.a: UL 2272 covers the electrical system of personal e-mobility devices. A listed scooter has passed standardized fire and electrical safety tests — worth prioritizing for daily charging at home.

scooter.q: What do smart scooter features actually do?
scooter.a: Companion apps often provide ride history, firmware updates, GPS tracking, remote lock, and anti-theft alarms. They help recovery and maintenance but do not replace a physical lock.

shared.q: How should I read the safety score?
shared.a: The score weights brakes, lights, reflectors, UL battery certification, and speed limiter. Higher is better for a young rider, but it is a checklist summary — not a crash prediction.
Landed price is item price plus estimated shipping to your door. We pick the lowest landed source as the default buy link when multiple stores are listed.

shared.q: How heavy is too heavy for a 12-year-old to carry?
shared.a: Under 40 lb is usually solo-carry territory for a strong tween. 41–55 lb is manageable with two hands but awkward on stairs. Above 55 lb, plan on adult help for curbs, cars, and storage. E-bikes are almost always heavier than a pedal beach cruiser.
